intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are indistinguishable or absent. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable specimen, anchored by the white rabbit.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only dark, undisturbed soil. No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot shows no signs of Mexican Mint. Appears to be empty soil.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen is present. Shows a single, thin, elongated stem with minimal leaf surface area. Health is poor/stunted.
plant_audit.P4: Merged with P2/P3 area; no distinct plant matter identified.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 area, serving as a consistent marker.
biome_observations.anomalies: No incidental growth or moss observed. The environment appears sterile of secondary life.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Minimal change; Pothos stem remains static.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: Significant lighting shift (blue-spectrum dominance) makes visual assessment difficult, but no growth or recovery is observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. The Pothos (P3) is the only surviving specimen, exhibiting signs of etiolation and extreme stress. The other pots are effectively fallow.
anomalies: Extreme lighting shift to blue-spectrum in later images
Absence of expected species in P1, P2, and P4
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is suffering from a catastrophic failure of maintenance. Only one specimen (Pothos) remains visible, and it is in a state of advanced physiological decline. The soil appears to be devoid of any other life, and the recent shift to blue-spectrum lighting suggests an attempt to force growth that is not manifesting. The environment is effectively a graveyard for the registered species.
